73936.subject: Automatická správa: Notifikácia o úhrade diaľničnej známky
73936.body:
Notifikácia o úhrade diaľničnej známky pre vozidlo s evidenčným číslom vozidla LE066BI 
  
  
 Vážený Zákazník, 
  
 správca výberu úhrady diaľničných známok za užívanie vymedzených úsekov diaľnic a rýchlostných ciest v Slovenskej republike, Vám prostredníctvom tejto automaticky vygenerovanej e-mailovej správy na základe Vami realizovanej úhrady diaľničnej známky, evidovanej v rámci evidencie úhrad diaľničných známok k vozidlu/prípojnému vozidlu s evidenčným číslom vozidla LE066BI a krajinou registrácie Slovensko, zasiela doklad „Potvrdenie o úhrade diaľničnej známky, zjednodušená faktúra". 
Diaľničná známka je platná v období odo dňa 02.06.2026 do dňa 02.06.2026 vrátane. 
  
 Upozornenie: 
  
 
Na túto automaticky vygenerovanú e-mailovú správu neodpovedajte. Bližšie informácie týkajúce sa diaľničnej známky môžete získať na internetovom portáli www.eznamka.sk a/alebo prostredníctvom zákazníckej linky na tel. čísle +421 232 777 777. 
  
 S pozdravom
 
  
 Národná diaľničná spoločnosť, a.s. 
  
 T: +421 232 777 777
W: www.eznamka.sk

Fear and Greed is at
29
(Fear)
and BTC has slipped to
$69K
this morning, extending yesterday's slide after Strategy
disclosed its first Bitcoin sale since 2022. The market is
testing levels not seen since April.

The session is mixed. HYPE hit a new all-time high even as BTC
fell. Anthropic filed for a US IPO ahead of OpenAI. Binance
opened thousands of US stocks to global users. And Toncoin is
reverting to a name that carries a complicated regulatory
history.
Pressure on BTC, momentum elsewhere. Let's dive in.

Latest News
-----------

Anthropic Just Filed for a US IPO Ahead of OpenAI

Anthropic submitted a confidential S-1 draft to the SEC on
June 1, becoming the first major AI lab to file for a US IPO
ahead of OpenAI.

The company raised funds at a valuation near $965 billion in
late May. Its valuation has more than doubled since February.

What was Anthropic's valuation when it raised funds just three
months ago in February?

Read More  
( https://link.coinmarketcap.com/f/a/Jb4DoTzB7rrBYG6d9Omk3A~~/AAQRxRA~/Wy8l9l3R_yhELHeHySDFIs8OsWosNpLnhdWaWyiZpJpFHJE0Vp13h-c2PxOuvwYEK98ztE4nsmXFaWkyb8dEIahyclSkSW_Xipk5W3tBFSB3xnhJ8TA-zZF9gnkM2vwMma-OpnGLGrUlZyt3O1YZlpBtpHYyR2PQAk0okCGl8P8~ )
   Binance Just Opened Thousands of US Stocks to Global Users

Binance launched trading in more than 7,000 US-listed stocks
and ETFs for non-US customers on June 1, with zero-commission
trades funded b

73924.subject: Reminder: Acceptance of Autopay Mobility Terms of Use Required
73924.body:
Autopay [https://mailing.autopay.eu/email_marketing_click.html?x=a62e&co=Sn3P&lc=oN&mc=o&s=45ty&u=t&z=gwVptWF&] 
Reminder: Acceptance of Autopay Mobility Terms of Use Required





Dear Autopay customer,

We would like to inform you about the new terms of use for Autopay Mobility, covering changes to billing rules and an expanded range of available services.






What do you need to do?
To continue using paid services in the Autopay app, please accept the new Price List.
Until your consent is provided, automatic services (e.g. motorway tolls, automatic parking) will be suspended.

I accept the new terms [https://app.autopay.pl/regulation-acceptance/8e52ddc72cb34af597e041e6e01c8bae] 




What is changing?

We are expanding the range of services
We are launching city parking payments in over 100 cities in Poland via the Autopay app.
Pay for parking with one click - no cash, no parking meters, and no need to remember to extend your parking time. All charges and billing history will be available in one place together with your other Autopay services.

We are standardizing billing rules for all users
From that date, service fees will be charged regardless of the number of vehicles added. The current rules that excluded fees for accounts with one vehicle will no longer apply.

We are adjusting the invoice delivery method for business users
We have introduced changes to how invoices are delivered to business users so that the process complies with applicable legal regulations and the requirements of the National e-Invoicing System (KSeF).





The full content of the new terms of use can be found in the Terms and Conditions [https://mailing.autopay.eu/email_marketing_click.html?x=a62e&co=Sn3P&lc=oM&mc=o&s=45ty&u=t&z=gWnasRl&] and Price List [https://mailing.autopay.eu/email_marketing_click.html?x=a62e&co=Sn3P&lc=oR&mc=o&s=45ty&u=t&z=gAS63eX&].
Please also note that in connection with the change to the Autopay terms of use, as a consumer you have the right to terminate the agreement in accordance with the rules set out in the Terms and Conditions.
If you have any questions about these changes, please visit the Help section or contact our support team.





Best regards,
Autopay Team


This message has been sent in accordance with the Autopay terms and conditions, by Autopay Mobility Sp. z o.o. with its registered office in Sopot, ul. Powstańców Warszawy 6, 81-718 Sopot, registered in the District Court Gdańsk-Północ, 8th Commercial Division of the National Court Register under No. 0000794787, NIP 527-28-98-696, REGON 383879060. Share capital PLN 19,296,050.00. If you are not the intended recipient of this message, please notify us and delete the message without opening any attachments.

73916.subject: Strategy's First Bitcoin Sale Since 2022 Trips a Polymarket Oracle
73916.body:
**gm, Defiers!**

* **[Strategy](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/strategy-sells-32-bitcoin-fund-preferred-dividends-first-sale-since-2022)**[ sells 32 BTC, its first disclosed sale since 2022](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/strategy-sells-32-bitcoin-fund-preferred-dividends-first-sale-since-2022)

* **[$60M Polymarket](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/usd85m-polymarket-dispute-over-strategy-s-may-bitcoin-sale-puts-uma-s-token-voting-oracle-on)**[ contract on Strategy's sale heads to UMA dispute](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/usd85m-polymarket-dispute-over-strategy-s-may-bitcoin-sale-puts-uma-s-token-voting-oracle-on)

* **[Gnosis Pay](https://thedefiant.io/news/hacks/gnosis-pay-hit-by-delay-module-exploit-as-gnosis-pledges-to-cover-user-losses)**[ hit by 'Delay Module' exploit; Gnosis covers losses](https://thedefiant.io/news/hacks/gnosis-pay-hit-by-delay-module-exploit-as-gnosis-pledges-to-cover-user-losses)

* **[Anchorage Digital](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/anchorage-digital-coordinated-multiparty-settlement-institutional-defi)**[ opens federal-charter settlement for non-custodial DeFi](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/anchorage-digital-coordinated-multiparty-settlement-institutional-defi)

* **[Binance](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/binance-opens-us-stock-trading-non-us-users-bstocks-bnb-chain)**[ opens US stock trading to non-US users, plans bStocks](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/binance-opens-us-stock-trading-non-us-users-bstocks-bnb-chain)

**Strategy** filed an 8-K Monday showing the [disposal of 32 BTC between May 26 and May 31 at an average $77,135 a coin](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/strategy-sells-32-bitcoin-fund-preferred-dividends-first-sale-since-2022), with proceeds earmarked for distributions on its STRC preferred stock. Small in absolute terms, the sale matters because it is the first disclosed Strategy disposal since the December 2022 tax-loss harvest. [The pivot we flagged last week](https://thedefiant.io/newsletter/ethereum-s-stake-queue-outpaces-exits-53-000-to-1) — Saylor adding bond exposure to a portfolio that had been a pure Bitcoin-treasury anchor — stopped being theoretical.

The same news immediately broke the [$60 million Polymarket contract](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/usd85m-polymarket-dispute-over-strategy-s-may-bitcoin-sale-puts-uma-s-token-voting-oracle-on) that bet on it. The market question — 'Will Strategy sell any bitcoin before May 31?' — was disputed twice and is now in front of **UMA** tokenholders. The narrow question is whether a 32-BTC disposal earmarked for preferred-stock dividends counts as 'selling bitcoin' under the contract's terms. The strategic question is whether UMA's token-voting oracle can settle high-stakes factual claims when the underlying event is itself ambiguous.

**Read more below!**

———————————————————————————

73912.subject: 🟪 The persistent inefficiency of finance
73912.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/666ae944-5cae-4c6c-abd6-970020388daf/BD-Banner-Sponsor_Template.png?t=1780094904)
Follow image link: (https://peaq.xyz)
Caption: 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/cf148e43-60a3-40ae-a8db-c5feb0b5a487/BD-Quote-Template-1.png?t=1780349421)
Caption: 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/e61ef14f-4e1e-4da0-81f2-c7a52189f1cb/Screenshot_2026-06-01_at_5.31.19_PM.png?t=1780349495)
Caption: 

# The persistent inefficiency of finance

To finance expansion plans in 1890, a US railroad company would typically hire a New York investment bank to underwrite the sale of stocks and bonds to investors. A prospectus would be printed with a mechanical press, probably using heavy paper stock and Victorian typography. There might be a fold-out map displaying the route of the tracks to be built. Many of these would be sent to London — by steamship — where partner banks would redistribute them to prospective investors, by hand or in the post. Interested investors would place their orders by handwritten note or in person; maybe over lunch in the City. The London bank would send its investors’ aggregated demand to New York by telegraph. The New York bank would have specialist engravers print ornate stock certificates to send back to investors. Ownership was recorded in a bound leather book and updated by hand whenever the securities traded. Final settlement occurred via bills of exchange — again by steamship, in the case of London investors. 

Early finance was slow and costly.

To finance a new datacenter in 2026, an AI company will typically hire a New York investment bank to underwrite the sale of stocks, bonds, and private credit to investors. The prospectus will be made in Microsoft Office and emailed to prospective investors around the world. Commitments to invest will be returned electronically. New shares and certificates are created digitally. Final settlement occurs by bank wire. Ownership is recorded in a database at the DTCC, updating automatically whenever the securities change hands.

Modern finance is fast… and surprisingly costly: despite the advent of information technology, intermediating between companies and investors is probably no more efficient today than it was in 1890.

To test this, finance professor Thomas Philippon undertook the herculean task of estimating how the “unit cost of financial intermediation” in the US has changed over time. 

One might expect that computers, the internet, and digitized money would lower the cost of connecting companies with capital: surely sending a prospectus by email rather than steamship would make the finance industry more efficient?

One would be disappointed.

“The finance ind
